claimText: Sedition Replaced (Section 152): Sedition removed; penalises acts threatening sovereignty such as Secession, armed rebellion, subversive activities, promoting separatism, threats to unity and integrity of India. Organised Crime: Defined as continuing unlawful activity including kidnapping, extortion, contract killing, land grabbing, cybercrime, etc. Group-based Violence: Murder by a group on grounds of race, caste, community, sex, language, belief, etc.: Death or life imprisonment and fine. Grievous hurt: Up to 7 years imprisonment and fine. 7.2.2. BHARATIYA NAGARIK SURAKSHA SANHITA 2023. It replaces the Criminal Procedure Code, 1973 (CrPC), which provided for the procedure for arrest, prosecution, and bail under various Acts. Aspect | Key Points. Background: First enacted in 1861; revised Codes in 1872 and 1882. Major amendments in 1898, 1923, 1955. CrPC, 1973 enacted based on 41st Law Commission Report. Detention of Undertrials: First-time offender released on bond after completing 1/3rd of maximum sentence. Bail mandatory if 1/2 of maximum sentence completed. Not applicable to offences punishable with death, life imprisonment, or multiple pending cases. Medical Examination: Police may seek medical examination of accused, including in rape cases. Forensic Investigation: Mandatory for offences punishable with 7 years or more imprisonment. Specimen Collection: Magistrate empowered to order signatures, fingerprints, and voice samples. Procedural Timelines: Prescribes timelines for medical reports, investigation progress updates to victims, framing of charges, and pronouncement of judgments. 7.2.3. BHARATIYA SAKSHYA ADHINIYAM 2023. It replaces Indian Evidence Act, 1872 (IEA), which governed the admissibility of evidence in Indian Courts in all civil and criminal proceedings. Aspect | Key Points. Background: Indian Evidence Act, 1872 enacted to consolidate laws relating to evidence. Electronic Evidence: Electronic/digital records have the same legal validity as paper records. Oral Evidence: Permits oral evidence through electronic means, including witness statements before courts. Joint Trials: Trials involving multiple accused treated as joint trials, even if one ...
